The app brings the traditional pen pal experience to your mobile device that mailing time depends on the distance between you and your friend, ranging from a few hours to days. Take your time to write and make your letter worth the wait.

Share your passion, learn a new culture or language, start connecting with people around the world on Slowly!

results.title: ‎Slowly: Make Global Friends App - App Store
results.author: JSNRXBRTS
highlights: Slowly: Build Authentic Friendships at Your Own Pace "In a world dominated by instant messaging, meaningful connections have become a rare luxury." Slowly reimagines the art of correspondence, offering a unique way to make friends. Connect with penpals around the world and explore the beauty of cultural and linguistic exchange through thoughtfully written letters. Rediscover the joy of anticipation and dive into heartfelt, written conversations. Designed for those who prefer to take their time and focus on genuine connections, Slowly brings back the charm of traditional penpals. Each letter takes time to arrive—anywhere from a few hours to a few days—depending on the distance between you and your new friend. Whether you’re looking for foreign friends, a language exchange partner, or simply a quiet space to write a meaningful letter, Slowly is here for you. Key Features: ► Distance-Based Letter Delivery Each letter travels at a speed that reflects the physical distance between you and your friend, creating a sense of anticipation. With no pressure to respond instantly, you have time to reflect, compose your thoughts, and share your story. This slower pace nurtures deeper and more meaningful connections. ► Collect Over 2,000 Unique Stamps Turn every letter into an adventure by collecting unique regional stamps from across the globe. These stamps add a personal and cultural touch to your correspondence, serving as reminders of the friendships you create. ► A Safe and Secure Space for Everyone No photos, no real names—just your thoughts, shared in a secure and stress-free environment. Whether you're an introvert looking for deeper conversations or someone who values privacy, Slowly offers a safe place to express yourself and connect authentically. ► Unlimited Letters, Always Free Enjoy the art of writing without limits—send and receive as many letters as you like, completely free. Optional premium features are available to enhance your experience and meet your unique needs. Who Is Slowly For? - Anyone looking to make friends at their own pace, free from the rush of instant communication. - Language learners seeking partners for meaningful language exchange. - People who love writing letters and want to explore different cultures. - Introverts and thoughtful individuals who prefer calm, meaningful interactions. - Anyone hoping to meet new friends from all over the world. Slowly: Authentic Friendships, at Your Speed. Whether you’re looking to reconnect with the joy of letter writing, discover new perspectives, or simply build friendships that matter, Slowly is your perfect companion for creating meaningful connections in a fast-paced world. Terms of Service: https://slowly.app/terms/

results.author: Mikilx
highlights: Slowly: Build Authentic Friendships at Your Own Pace "In a world dominated by instant messaging, meaningful connections have become a rare luxury." Slowly reimagines the art of correspondence, offering a unique way to make friends. Connect with penpals around the world and explore the beauty of cultural and linguistic exchange through thoughtfully written letters. Rediscover the joy of anticipation and dive into heartfelt, written conversations. Designed for those who prefer to take their time and focus on genuine connections, Slowly brings back the charm of traditional penpals. Each letter takes time to arrive—anywhere from a few hours to a few days—depending on the distance between you and your new friend. Whether you’re looking for foreign friends, a language exchange partner, or simply a quiet space to write a meaningful letter, Slowly is here for you. Key Features: ► Distance-Based Letter Delivery Each letter travels at a speed that reflects the physical distance between you and your friend, creating a sense of anticipation. With no pressure to respond instantly, you have time to reflect, compose your thoughts, and share your story. This slower pace nurtures deeper and more meaningful connections. ► Collect Over 2,000 Unique Stamps Turn every letter into an adventure by collecting unique regional stamps from across the globe. These stamps add a personal and cultural touch to your correspondence, serving as reminders of the friendships you create. ► A Safe and Secure Space for Everyone No photos, no real names—just your thoughts, shared in a secure and stress-free environment. Whether you're an introvert looking for deeper conversations or someone who values privacy, Slowly offers a safe place to express yourself and connect authentically. ► Unlimited Letters, Always Free Enjoy the art of writing without limits—send and receive as many letters as you like, completely free. Optional premium features are available to enhance your experience and meet your unique needs. Who Is Slowly For? - Anyone looking to make friends at their own pace, free from the rush of instant communication. - Language learners seeking partners for meaningful language exchange. - People who love writing letters and want to explore different cultures. - Introverts and thoughtful individuals who prefer calm, meaningful interactions. - Anyone hoping to meet new friends from all over the world. Slowly: Authentic Friendships, at Your Speed. Whether you’re looking to reconnect with the joy of letter writing, discover new perspectives, or simply build friendships that matter, Slowly is your perfect companion for creating meaningful connections in a fast-paced world. Terms of Service: https://slowly.app/terms/

highlights:
# Paxmeet: The Next Social Media of Events
[...]
India's offline experience ecosystem is here. KYC-verified meetups, real rooms, real people. Paxmeet is the next social media of events.
[...]
Paxmeet is building India's first true **offline experience and events ecosystem** — a vibe-based social platform where verified, like-minded people discover, host, and live through real moments together. House parties that go till sunrise. Café conversations that last six hours. Beachside jams. Creative workshops.
[...]
On Paxmeet, the feed isn't reels. It's plans. Real ones. Tonight, this weekend, next Friday. You open the app and see a curated map of energy near you: parties, gigs, hikes, dinners, screenings, gaming nights, sports meets, sound baths, art jams, run clubs. Every event is **vibe-tagged** so you find your kind of room — chill, wild, creative, social, romantic, athletic, intellectual, introvert-friendly.
[...]

— government ID, photo match, phone, sometimes social vouching.
[...]
25 burner profiles from the same person. When you walk into a Paxmeet event, every face in that room has been verified
[...]
Instagram tells the world what you posted. LinkedIn tells the world what you worked. **Paxmeet tells the world how you show up.**
[...]
Instagram is a content network. LinkedIn is a career network. WhatsApp is a messaging network. **Paxmeet is the experience network,** the social graph of who you actually spend real time with, where, doing what, with whom.
[...]
That's a graph no existing platform owns. And it's the most valuable graph of the next decade. Because as AI floods the internet with synthetic everything, the rarest, most premium thing in the world becomes **verified human presence in a real room.** That's exactly what Paxmeet sells, structures, and scales.
[...]
What is Paxmeet?Paxmeet is India's offline experience and events ecosystem — a vibe-based social platform where KYC-verified, like-minded people discover, host, and join real-world meetups, parties, gigs, workshops, and community gatherings near them.
[...]
How is Paxmeet different from Meetup or Eventbrite?Meetup and Eventbrite are event listings. Paxmeet is the next social media of events — a full ecosystem combining KYC-verified profiles, vibe-based discovery, vendor curation, creator partnerships, artist management, community building, event group chats, and the largest dedicated ad network for the events industry.
[...]
Who is Paxmeet for?Paxmeet is for anyone who wants real-world experiences over endless scrolling — Gen Z, millennials, hosts, creators, independent artists, brands, vendors, and communities. If you host events, attend events, or build experiences, Paxmeet is built for you.

Pie: Download the app and set your city — Pie is live in Chicago, Austin, Bay Area, and Columbus. Your home feed shows free, in-person events happening nearby, filtered by your interests. Tap an event to see who's going, RSVP, and add it to your calendar. After you attend, Pie starts learning who you vibe with and surfaces a personalized feed of friend and friend-of-friend activity. On 222: 222 is one of the more interesting approaches to IRL social I've seen. By removing profiles and messaging entirely, they've eliminated the part of friendship apps that feels most like work. The personality matching and curated evenings mean you show up, meet cool people, and go somewhere fun — all without planning anything. The catch is availability: it's iOS-only and in just a few cities, so if you're not in NYC, LA, SF, or Chicago, you're out of luck for now. If you are, though, it's worth trying at least once.
[...]
On Pie: Pie is refreshingly simple in a space full of personality quizzes and subscription paywalls. The pitch is: here are free events near you, go to them, meet people. That's it. No matching algorithm, no premium tier, no gamification. The friend-of-friend social graph that builds over time is genuinely clever — it mimics how real-life social circles actually form. The catch is geographic: four cities is a small footprint, and if yours isn't on the list, you're waiting. But if you're in Chicago, Austin, or the Bay Area and want a zero-cost way to build a social life, Pie is the obvious first download.

results.title: Meet5: Funding, Team & Investors | Startup Intros
results.author: Startup Intros
highlights:
Meet5: Funding, Team & Investors | Startup Intros
[...]
Meet5 is an app designed to get you off your phone. Find, create, and participate in group activities and make new friends.
[...]
Meet5 is a Frankfurt-based social app that organizes group activities for adults, primarily targeting people over 40 to combat loneliness through real-life connections.[1][2][3] It builds a platform where users discover and join local events like hikes, dinners, trivia nights, and brunches, turning online matches into offline group experiences; it serves an underserved demographic of Gen X and Boomers (average user age 57) who seek meaningful friendships post-life changes like empty nests.[1][2][3] The app solves the loneliness epidemic—highlighted by a WHO study noting one in six people affected—by prioritizing low-pressure group settings over one-on-one swiping, with users attending an average of four events monthly and the platform hosting 2.5 million members, 40,000+ activities, and 300,000 monthly participants across Europe.[1][2][3] Monetized via a $12.50/month premium subscription for features like private messaging and filtered events, Meet5 achieved profitability by mid-2024, raised €8 million ($9 million) Series A from Peak in 2025 for US expansion (launched summer 2025), and doubled its team to 80 employees.[1][2]
[...]
Meet5 was founded in 2017 in Frankfurt, Germany, by Lukas Reinhardt (CEO) and Kai Burghardt as Go Crush, a dating app focused on group meetings to reduce first-date awkwardness.[1][3][4] The duo pivoted at the end of 2019 to emphasize platonic group experiences for users over 40, capitalizing on demand for community amid shifting social needs.[1][2] Early traction built steadily over eight years in Western Europe, evolving into Europe's top social gathering app with thriving metrics before profitability in mid-2024 and the 2025 Series A to fuel global growth.[1][2][3]
[...]
- Group-centric, IRL-first model: Matches users into casual group activities around shared interests (e.g., coffee chats, cultural events, travel), emphasizing community comfort over solo dating or swiping; tech drives users "off the screen" with personalized feeds and smart recommendations.[2][3][5]

meeTalk Global Chat is a next-generation language exchange and social messaging platform that connects you with native speakers from around the world. The app enables real-time matching based on your interests, target languages, and proficiency level, helping you meet new people and learn naturally through authentic conversations. results.title: Meet5: Funding, Team & Investors | Startup Intros
results.author: Startup Intros
highlights:
Meet5 is an app designed to get you off your phone. Find, create, and participate in group activities and make new friends.
[...]
Meet5 is a Frankfurt-based social app that organizes group activities for adults, primarily targeting people over 40 to combat loneliness through real-life connections.[1][2][3] It builds a platform where users discover and join local events like hikes, dinners, trivia nights, and brunches, turning online matches into offline group experiences; it serves an underserved demographic of Gen X and Boomers (average user age 57) who seek meaningful friendships post-life changes like empty nests.[1][2][3] The app solves the loneliness epidemic—highlighted by a WHO study noting one in six people affected—by prioritizing low-pressure group settings over one-on-one swiping, with users attending an average of four events monthly and the platform hosting 2.5 million members, 40,000+ activities, and 300,000 monthly participants across Europe.[1][2][3] Monetized via a $12.50/month premium subscription for features like private messaging and filtered events, Meet5 achieved profitability by mid-2024, raised €8 million ($9 million) Series A from Peak in 2025 for US expansion (launched summer 2025), and doubled its team to 80 employees.[1][2]

- Age-specific focus on 40+ users: Unlike broad apps like Meetup or newer rivals (222, Timeleft, Pie), Meet5 caters to older adults seeking to "feel young again" via tailored group events, fostering loyalty with high engagement (4 events/user/month).[1][2]
[...]
- Group-centric, IRL-first model: Matches users into casual group activities around shared interests (e.g., coffee chats, cultural events, travel), emphasizing community comfort over solo dating or swiping; tech drives users "off the screen" with personalized feeds and smart recommendations.[2][3][5]

results.title: 222 vs Pie: Side-by-Side Comparison (2026) — Søren
highlights:
222 and Pie are both friendship apps that help you meet people in real life, but they take different approaches. Here’s how they stack up across pricing, format, cities, and more.
[...]
Pie: Download the app and set your city — Pie is live in Chicago, Austin, Bay Area, and Columbus. Your home feed shows free, in-person events happening nearby, filtered by your interests. Tap an event to see who's going, RSVP, and add it to your calendar. After you attend, Pie starts learning who you vibe with and surfaces a personalized feed of friend and friend-of-friend activity. You can also host your own events — keep them private or broadcast them to the whole network using 'snowball mode.' There's a built-in chat for coordinating plans and sharing photos after the fact.
[...]
222: No profiles, no DMs, no swiping — removes all the friction and awkwardness of typical social apps. Personality-based matching means you're not just thrown in with random strangers. Full evening experiences (dinner + activity) feel like a real night out, not a forced meetup. All members are vetted before being selected for events. Strong TikTok community and word-of-mouth reputation in major cities.
[...]
Completely free — no premium
[...]
paywalls,
[...]
catch. Event-first model
[...]
you're bonding over shared experiences, not forced small talk. The friend-of-friend feed creates organic social discovery that feels natural. You can host your own events, giving you control over the vibe. 4.7-star rating with 1,000+ reviews suggests people genuinely love using it.
[...]
On 222: 222 is one of the more interesting approaches to IRL social I've seen. By removing profiles and messaging entirely, they've eliminated the part of friendship apps that feels most like work. The personality matching and curated evenings mean you show up, meet cool people, and go somewhere fun — all without planning anything. The catch is availability: it's iOS-only and in just a few cities, so if you're not in NYC, LA, SF, or Chicago, you're out of luck for now. If you are, though, it's worth trying at least once.
[...]
On Pie: Pie is refreshingly simple in a space full of personality quizzes and subscription paywalls. The pitch is: here are free events near you, go to them, meet people. That's it. No matching algorithm, no premium tier, no gamification. The friend-of-friend social graph that builds over time is genuinely clever — it mimics how real-life social circles actually form. The catch is geographic: four cities is a small footprint, and if yours isn't on the list, you're waiting. But if you're in Chicago, Austin, or the Bay Area and want a zero-cost way to build a social life, Pie is the obvious first download.

results.title: WeRoad Secures $58M Investment from Airbnb to Improve Its U.S. Group Travel Platform - Tech Weekly
results.author: Lauren Schultz
highlights:
WeRoad, a Milan-based startup focused on group travel, has secured an impressive $58 million in a Series C funding round led by Airbnb. This marks their initial significant venture outside Europe. The raised funds, totaling nearly $100 million, will facilitate WeRoad’s expansion into the U.S. market, beginning with Austin.
[...]
As WeRoad moves forward with its U.S. expansion, it caters to increasing concerns about loneliness among younger generations. This issue has emerged as both a critical public health concern and a practical business opportunity. While numerous tech firms are shifting towards AI, WeRoad is embracing the burgeoning “IRL economy,” which emphasizes offline interactions over digital ones. Other initiatives like Timeleft, 222, and Pie are similarly exploring community-driven and event-focused experiences.
[...]
WeRoad is also broadening its offerings beyond just travel. In 2025, the company introduced WeMeet, an app dedicated to local events, featuring activities such as communal dinners, hiking, yoga sessions, running clubs, after-work meetups, and game nights. Last year, WeMeet attracted over 50,000 participants across 35 cities and achieved 150,000 downloads.
[...]
WeRoad plans to integrate WeMeet into its U.S. strategy, focusing on select cities rather than a nationwide launch, starting in Austin by recruiting group leaders, organizing local events, and building community partnerships before further expansion.
[...]
“We aspire to host WeMeet events in various U.S. cities in 2026, beginning with Austin, known for its vibrant culture and active community,” added De Nadai.

results.title: WeRoad raises $58 Mn to expand social travel platform into the U.S. | Business Review Live | Business News, Reviews | Entrepreneur Stories, Interviews | Kerala | India
results.author: BRL Editor
highlights:
WeRoad has raised $58 million in a Series C funding round led by Airbnb as the Milan-based group travel startup prepares for its first major expansion beyond Europe. Through the latest funding round, the company has increased its total capital raised to nearly $100 million and plans to use the fresh investment to accelerate its entry into the United States, starting with Austin.
[...]
While much of the technology industry currently prioritises artificial intelligence, WeRoad has positioned itself within the expanding “IRL economy,” a category of startups that monetise in-person interaction instead of digital screen time. Additionally, companies such as Timeleft, 222, and Pie are also exploring similar concepts through social dinners, clubs, local events, and community-focused gatherings.
[...]
Meanwhile, WeRoad has expanded beyond travel services by launching WeMeet in 2025, an app dedicated to local in-person gatherings, including dinners, yoga sessions, hikes, running clubs, after-work social events, and board game nights. The company stated that more than 50,000 people attended WeMeet events across 35 cities last year, while the app achieved over 150,000 downloads.
[...]
Furthermore, WeRoad plans to use WeMeet as a central pillar of its U.S. expansion strategy. Instead of immediately expanding nationwide, the company intends to focus on a select group of cities beginning with Austin, where it will recruit group leaders, organise local events, and establish community partnerships before scaling operations further.
[...]
“We’ll be launching WeMeet events across multiple U.S. cities throughout 2026, starting with Austin because of its incredible energy and vibrant community scene,” De Nadai said.
